title: Post-Training Assessment
courseTopic: Behavioral Assessment in ABA: Core Tools and Considerations

choices.text: The Visual Performance and Matching Assessment Program
The Verbal Behavior Milestones Assessment and Placement Program
The Verbal Behavior and Monitoring Assessed Program

questions.text: A correct example of an "Intraverbal" skill is:

choices.text: The child says "yes" when the therapist asks, "Do you want a cookie?"
The child says, "I don't want the ball."
The child says, "Wow! That's a big car!"
The child says "yes" when the therapist asks, "Did you go to school today?"

questions.text: The possible score for each item on the VB-MAPP assessment is:

choices.text: Score from 1 to 4 depends on the skill

questions.text: Which assessment tool evaluates the Self-Care domain using a separate subtest rather than as one of its primary domains?

You are scoring the Labeling domain in the ABLLS-R. The child correctly named 3 items. Based on the scoring criteria below, what score should be assigned?
Scoring criteria:
* Score "1" = Names at least 2 items.
* Score "2" = Names at least 4 items.
* Score "3" = Names at least 6 items.
* Score "4" = Names 10 or more items.
You are conducting an assessment session and are going to assess the Tact, Writing, and Motor Imitation domains. Which of the following should you consider?

choices.text: Assess all tact skills first, then all writing skills, followed by all motor imitation skills
Assume the child's performance in each skill and score it without direct observation of his performance
Intersperse skills from different domains to avoid boredom and to maintain child's motivation

questions.text: When writing an assessment report and explaining results, you should:

choices.text: Present strengths before areas for improvement (weaknesses)
Present all skills together without a specific order

questions.text:
You read an assessment report that stated:
 "The child was able to follow three-step instructions, select actions, select adjectives, and respond to his name."
 What should be corrected in this report?

choices.text: Present the simpler skills first, followed by the more advanced skills, to maintain a logical order
The report is correct and does not require any revisions

questions.text: When writing the Background Information section, which order of information is the most appropriate?

choices.text: The child's name, then the previous interventions, followed by the diagnosis and medical history
The child's name, age and diagnosis first, followed by the medical history, then the level of language, and finally the previous interventions
No specific order is  needed; any order is acceptable
The diagnosis first, followed by the age, after that what the family want us to do
